facts.text: Template lives at examples/nextjs-quickstart/ in the repo root.
It is a standard Next.js App Router project with TypeScript.
Authentication is API-key only — no Clerk, Better Auth, or session auth. The API key is set via SQUIDCRAWL_API_KEY environment variable.
A shared SDK client module (lib/squidcrawl.ts) creates and exports a DeepcrawlApp instance configured with the API key and base URL from environment variables.
Route Handlers (app/api/squidcrawl/*/route.ts) expose each SDK method as a POST endpoint that the frontend calls.
A /url section on the demo page takes a URL + options and calls readUrl — displaying title, cleaned HTML, metadata.
A /markdown section takes a URL and displays the raw markdown output from getMarkdown in a code block.
A /links section takes a URL, calls extractLinks, and renders the hierarchical link tree and total count.
A /logs section fetches recent activity logs via listLogs and displays them in a paginated table.
A /log-detail section takes a log ID, calls getOneLog, and shows the full request/response details.
A /export section takes a log ID and format (json/markdown/links), calls exportResponse, and displays the exported result.
The demo page uses Tailwind CSS for layout and shadcn/ui components (Button, Input, Card, Tabs, Badge) with loading spinners and error boundaries.
The page is a single-page demo with a header, tab/section navigation for each SDK feature, and a results area per section.
A README.md explains prerequisites (Node 20+, API key), setup steps, available features, and links to full docs.
A .env.example file documents the required SQUIDCRAWL_API_KEY and optional NEXT_PUBLIC_SQUIDCRAWL_API_URL variables.
The template can be run standalone (cd examples/nextjs-quickstart && pnpm install && pnpm dev) without the rest of the monorepo.
Source files are heavily commented to serve as documentation — each Route Handler and component explains the SDK method being used.
